# Ethan Gunderson — ethangunderson.com

## About

Ethan Gunderson is a principal software engineer at Cars Commerce with twenty years of experience building software. He specializes in observability, performance engineering, and Elixir/Erlang systems. He is writing a book on instrumenting Elixir applications with Telemetry. He founded ChicagoDB and Papers We Love Chicago, hosted the Growing Software podcast, and has spoken at Elixir conferences.

Off the clock he is a dad to two boys, an avid cook, a BJJ white belt, and an enthusiast of specialty coffee, stationery, and well-made objects.

## Content Areas

### Technical Writing (/notes)

Posts on observability, SLOs, SLAs, Elixir, Telemetry, software engineering practices, and conference talks. Representative topics:
- The difference between SLOs and SLAs (and why it matters in production systems)
- Telemetry-powered observability in Elixir applications
- Software engineering career and craft

### Specialty Coffee Reviews (/coffee)

Ethan reviews single-origin specialty coffees from independent roasters with a 1–7 rating scale. Each review includes: roaster, origin region, processing method, roast level, brew notes, and a numeric rating. The rating scale is documented at /my-rating-system.

**Rating Scale (1–7):**
- 7: Perfect, must try, life-changing
- 6: Excellent, worth repeating — go out of your way
- 5: Good, enjoyable
- 4: Passable — fine but wouldn't repurchase
- 3: Bad — avoid if you can
- 2: Atrocious — actively repulsive
- 1: Evil — life-changing in a bad way

Roasters reviewed include: PERC Coffee, The Roasted Record, Drink Coffee Do Stuff, Portrait Coffee, Ruby Coffee Roasters, and others.

### Now (/now)

What Ethan is currently reading, building, and thinking about.

### Atlas (/atlas)

Personal travel notes and cities visited.

### Projects (/projects)

Notable software projects and community work.
